words.def: the group of people who are responsible for controlling a country or a state
words.example: to lead/form a government

words.def: a common wild plant with narrow green leaves and stems that are eaten by cows, horses, sheep, etc.
words.example: a blade of grass

words.def: to say hello to somebody or to welcome them
words.example: greet somebody, He greeted all the guests warmly as they arrived.

words.def: the solid surface of the earth
words.example: on the ground, I found her lying on the ground.

words.def: a person that you have invited to your house or to a particular event that you are paying for
words.example: They only use the dining room when they have guests.

words.def: a weapon that is used for firing bullets or shells
words.example: I have never fired a gun in my life.

words.def: a man
words.example: a big/little guy

words.def: a thing that you do often and almost without thinking, especially something that is hard to stop doing
words.example: You need to change your eating habits.

words.def: a space or passage inside the entrance or front door of a building
words.example: in a hall, Her brother was standing in the front hall.

words.def: in a cheerful way; with feelings of pleasure
words.example: children playing happily on the beach

words.def: to own, hold or possess something
words.example: He had a new car and a boat.

words.def: a continuous pain in the head
words.example: He had a terrible headache.

words.def: the organ in the chest that sends blood around the body, usually on the left in humans
words.example: The patient's heart stopped beating for a few seconds.

words.def: the quality of being hot
words.example: Heat rises.

words.def: weighing a lot; difficult to lift or move
words.example: a heavy weight/load

words.def: the act of helping somebody to do something
words.example: Thank you for all your help.

words.def: able to improve a particular situation
words.example: helpful advice/information

words.def: a person who is admired by many people for doing something brave or good
words.example: a war hero (= somebody who was very brave during a war)

words.def: of or belonging to her
words.example: His eyes met hers.

words.def: used when the woman or girl who performs an action is also affected by it
words.example: She hurt herself.

words.def: used to attract somebody’s attention or to express interest, surprise or anger
words.example: Hey, can I just ask you something?

words.def: to put or keep somebody/something in a place where they/it cannot be seen or found
words.example: hide somebody/something + adv./prep., He hid the letter in a drawer.

words.def: an area of land that is higher than the land around it, but not as high as a mountain
words.example: a region of gently rolling hills

words.def: used when the man or boy who performs an action is also affected by it
words.example: He introduced himself.

words.def: of or belonging to a man or boy who has already been mentioned or is easily identified
words.example: James has sold his car.

words.def: a game played on a field by two teams of 11 players, with curved sticks and a small hard ball. Teams try to hit the ball into the other team’s goal.
words.example: to play hockey

words.def: a hollow space in something solid or in the surface of something
words.example: He dug a deep hole in the garden.

words.def: connected with the place where you live
words.example: home life (= with your family)

words.def: a feeling of wanting and expecting a particular thing to happen; something that you wish for
words.example: Don't lose hope—we'll find her.

words.def: used to introduce a statement that contrasts with something that has just been said
words.example: He was feeling bad. He went to work, however, and tried to concentrate.

words.def: of or connected with people rather than animals, machines or gods
words.example: the human body/brain
One hundred (of the children) have already been placed with foster families.

words.def: suffering from an illness or disease; not feeling well
words.example: Her father is seriously ill in St Luke's hospital.

words.def: a picture, photograph or statue that represents somebody/something
words.example: Most simple leaflets will include text and images.

words.def: that cannot exist or be done; not possible
words.example: almost/virtually/nearly impossible

words.def: to become better than before; to make something/somebody better than before
words.example: Overall the situation has improved dramatically.

words.def: into an object, an area or a substance
words.example: She opened the door and went in.

words.def: if one thing includes another, it has the second thing as one of its parts
words.example: include something, The tour included a visit to the Science Museum.

words.def: contained as part of something
words.example: all of Europe (Britain included)

words.def: any small creature with six legs and a body divided into three parts. Insects usually also have wings. Ants, bees and flies are all insects.
words.example: Most birds spend most of the day eating insects.

words.def: in the place of somebody/something
words.example: Lee was ill so I went instead.

words.def: an object used for producing musical sounds, for example a piano or a drum
words.example: to play an instrument

words.def: good at learning, understanding and thinking in a logical way about things; showing this ability
words.example: a highly intelligent child

words.def: the feeling that you have when you want to know or learn more about somebody/something
words.example: Her research has generated interest around the world.

words.def: connected with or involving two or more countries
words.example: international trade/law

words.def: to produce or design something that has not existed before
words.example: Who invented the steam engine?

words.def: a thing or an idea that has been invented
words.example: Fax machines were a wonderful invention at the time.

words.def: to ask somebody to come to a social event
words.example: invite somebody to something, Have you been invited to their party?

words.def: one thing on a list of things to buy, do, talk about, etc.
words.example: What's the next item on the agenda?

words.def: used when the animal or thing that does an action is also affected by it
words.example: The cat was washing itself.

words.def: a thick sweet substance made by boiling fruit with sugar, often sold in jars and spread on bread
words.example: strawberry jam

words.def: objects such as rings and necklaces that people wear as decoration
words.example: silver/gold jewellery

words.def: something that you say or do to make people laugh, for example a funny story that you tell
words.example: I can't tell jokes.

words.def: an act of travelling from one place to another, especially when they are far apart
words.example: They went on a long train journey across India.

words.def: a child or young person
words.example: Do you have any kids?

words.def: to make somebody/something die
words.example: kill (somebody/something), Cancer kills thousands of people every year.

words.def: a unit for measuring distance; 1 000 metres
words.example: The industrial estate is 6 kilometres from the city centre.

words.def: the male ruler of an independent state that has a royal family
words.example: the kings and queens of England

words.def: the joint between the top and bottom parts of the leg where it bends in the middle
words.example: a knee injury

words.def: a sharp blade (= metal edge) with a handle, used for cutting or as a weapon
words.example: knives and forks

words.def: the information, understanding and skills that you gain through education or experience
words.example: scientific/technical knowledge and skills

words.def: a word used to mean ‘woman’ that some people, especially older people, consider is more polite
words.example: There's a lady waiting to see you.

words.def: a large area of water that is surrounded by land
words.example: in a lake, We swam in the lake.

words.def: a device that uses electricity, oil or gas to produce light
words.example: a desk/bedside lamp

words.def: a rule that deals with a particular crime, agreement, etc.
words.example: to pass a law (= officially make it part of the system of laws)

words.def: a person who is trained and qualified to advise people about the law and to represent them in court, and to write legal documents
words.example: to hire a lawyer

words.def: unwilling to work or be active; doing as little as possible
words.example: He was not stupid, just lazy.

words.def: a person who leads a group of people, especially the head of a country, an organization, etc.
words.example: party/world leaders

words.def: a yellow citrus fruit with juice that has a bitter, sharp taste. Slices of lemon and lemon juice are used in cooking and drinks.
words.example: Add a squeeze of lemon juice.

words.def: to give something to somebody or allow them to use something that belongs to you, which they have to return to you later
words.example: lend something, They will lend equipment free of charge.

words.def: to a smaller degree; not so much
words.example: less expensive/likely/intelligent

words.def: the way in which a person or a group of people lives and works
words.example: Many people are trying to adopt a healthy lifestyle these days.

words.def: pale in colour
words.example: He's got light blue eyes.

words.def: having a good chance of happening or being something; probable or expected
words.example: the most likely explanation/outcome/scenario

words.def: not big; small; smaller than others
words.example: a little house

words.def: a device that keeps a door, window, box, etc. shut, usually needing a key to open it
words.example: She turned the key in the lock.

words.def: an act of looking at somebody/something
words.example: look at somebody/something, Here, have a look at this.

words.def: to be unable to find something/somebody
words.example: I've lost my keys.

words.def: unable to find your way; not knowing where you are
words.example: We always get lost in London.

words.def: in a way that makes a lot of noise
words.example: She screamed as loudly as she could.

words.def: beautiful; attractive
words.example: She looked particularly lovely that night.
